title: A header row is not just the first row
why.no-th: The first row is still <td>. Meaning your code claims "Model" is just another value — like "18 leva". It is not. It is the NAME of the column. The tag is <th>.
why.still-td: One of the column names is still a <td>. All three must be <th>.
why.no-caption: The table has no name. Lift it out of the page — numbers with no explanation. <caption> is the first thing inside <table>.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
blocks.0.text: The table works. Three rows, three columns, everything in place.
blocks.1.text: But the first row is not like the others. "Model, Price, Wheels" are not data — they are the names of the columns. And you told them <td>: "just another cell with a value".
blocks.2.text: There is a tag for it
blocks.4.text: <th> — from "table header". It says: this is not a value, this is a column name.
blocks.6.text: Run it and look: the browser makes them bold and centres them by itself. Pleasant, but that is not the point.
blocks.7.text: The point is for the person who cannot see the table.
blocks.8.text: A blind person hears it cell by cell. When they reach "18 leva", their computer says: "Price: 18 leva". They know which column they are in without counting.
blocks.9.text: Without <th> they hear only: "18 leva". And wonder what that number is.
blocks.12.text: <caption> is the name of the table itself. It goes first, right after <table>.
blocks.13.text: It seems redundant — there is a heading above, is there not? But the heading belongs to the page, not the table. Lift the table out and put it elsewhere, and it must still say what it contains.
blocks.15.text: There is one question that settles everything:
blocks.16.text: Take a cell from the middle of the table. Not from the first row, not from the first column. Just a cell.
blocks.17.text: Can you say what it means? "18" — 18 what? Price? Kilograms? Quantity?
blocks.18.text: If your table has <th>, the answer is always there: "this is the price of the mountain bike". Every cell has two addresses — a row and a column.
blocks.19.text: If you cannot name them — you did not build a table. You built a grid.
blocks.20.text: Your turn. Make the first row a header row and give the table a name.
blocks.21.text: Run it and look: almost nothing changes on screen. Same as always — the most important work in HTML is invisible.
